attributes.action: Guarantee Specialist Endometriosis Care/Access and Mandatory Training in the UK
attributes.additional_details: Endometriosis affects 1.5 million people in the UK, yet access to specialist care is limited and uneven. Diagnosis takes an average of 8 years, and many patients are misdiagnosed or dismissed. The pain is often compared to kidney stones, appendicitis, or labour contractions, yet patients wait years for specialist review. Mandatory training and better access would reduce misdiagnosis, emergency visits, and long‑term complications.
attributes.background:
Ensure fair access to accredited specialist endometriosis centres.  
Introduce mandatory endometriosis training for GPs, nurses, and frontline clinicians.  
Improve referral pathways to reduce delays in diagnosis and treatment.  
Set UK‑wide standards for waiting times for specialist assessment.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petitions, which call for similar action:

Fund improvement to PCOS, Endometriosis, and Fertility Health care and treatment
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/757794

Set a maximum 6‑month diagnosis target for endometriosis
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/756697

Improve Monitoring and Early Detection for Endometriosis Patients in the UK
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/764219
